perturbation.original_text: 1.91 "Sales Representative" means a sales representative used by Company to perform Details of the Product for the Initial Indication to Health Care Professionals in the Territory. Sales Representative shall not include any key account manager, medical science liaison or regional sales manager.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.91 "Sales Representative" means a sales representative, also referred to as a 'marketing agent', used by Company to perform Details of the Product for the Initial Indication to Health Care Professionals in the Territory. Sales Representative shall not include any key account manager, medical science liaison or regional sales manager.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces 'marketing agent' as an interchangeable term for 'Sales Representative'. This creates potential confusion because while 'Sales Representative' is explicitly defined with limitations (excluding key account managers, etc.), 'marketing agent' is not, potentially broadening the scope of personnel covered by the obligations tied to 'Sales Representative.' This impacts the performance metrics and training requirements outlined elsewhere in the document. The term 'marketing agent' is never reffered to again to clarify if it is equal to sales representative, hence, creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3.1 Qualifications. Company shall ensure that each Sales Representative: (i) is a full-time employee of Company and a full-time member of its sales force; (ii) possesses skills, training and experience that are consistent with industry standards applicable to the promotion of an oncological pharmaceutical product; (iii) has completed the Product-Specific Training and other sales training described in this Agreement and the Brand Plan and achieved a Passing Score on an examination in accordance with Section 2.2.2(c); and (iv) has become adequately equipped and knowledgeable with respect to the Product, as determined in accordance with Company's then-current standards for sales personnel selling pharmaceutical products in the Territory. No sales representative or other individual may be used by Company to perform in-person presentations of the Product in the Territory unless and until such individual satisfies the conditions described in clauses (i) - (iv) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the term 'marketing agent' as potentially interchangeable with 'Sales Representative,' while the contradicted text (3.3.1) imposes specific qualifications (full-time employee, training, etc.) on 'Sales Representatives.' Because the Changed Text expands the definition of 'Sales Representative' to include an undefined 'marketing agent', this means that the obligations in 3.3.1 may not apply to the now defined 'marketing agent'. This creates a contradiction because the requirements that apply to a sales representative might not apply to a marketing agent, even though they are now the same thing.

perturbation.original_text: 3.4.2 Promotional Materials Development and Approval. Janssen shall be solely responsible for developing and providing to Company (at Janssen's cost) all promotional materials for use in connection with the Promotion of the Product (the "Promotional Materials") and agrees to provide Company with sufficient quantities of the materials based on market demand and expected levels of Detailing efforts. Janssen agrees to consider in good faith Company's feedback in the development of any new promotional materials during the Term. Such Promotional Materials shall comply with all applicable Laws and may include written sales and advertising materials, detail aids, brochures, hand-outs, reprints, booth panels and any other promotional support items. Company shall use only the Promotional Materials provided by Janssen and the Product Label and Insert in its Promotion of the Product in the Territory. Company shall not add any Company Trademark to the Promotional Materials or otherwise alter the Promotional Materials in any way. Company shall not develop or use any other promotional materials in its Promotion of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4.2 Promotional Materials Development and Approval. Janssen shall be solely responsible for developing and providing to Company (at Janssen's cost) all promotional materials, also reffered to as Marketing Toolkits, for use in connection with the Promotion of the Product (the "Promotional Materials") and agrees to provide Company with sufficient quantities of the materials based on market demand and expected levels of Detailing efforts. Janssen agrees to consider in good faith Company's feedback in the development of any new promotional materials during the Term. Such Promotional Materials shall comply with all applicable Laws and may include written sales and advertising materials, detail aids, brochures, hand-outs, reprints, booth panels and any other promotional support items. Company shall use only the Promotional Materials provided by Janssen and the Product Label and Insert in its Promotion of the Product in the Territory. Company shall not add any Company Trademark to the Promotional Materials or otherwise alter the Promotional Materials in any way. Company shall not develop or use any other promotional materials in its Promotion of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The added term 'Marketing Toolkits' as an interchangeable term for 'Promotional Materials' introduces ambiguity. While 'Promotional Materials' are strictly controlled (Janssen's responsibility, Company cannot alter), 'Marketing Toolkits' lack this explicit control. If 'Marketing Toolkits' are understood as something different, it could create avenues for Company to develop and use additional marketing materials, which is prohibited by the end of the paragraph and in other sections. This contradiction generates uncertainty around the scope of permitted promotional activities and materials, hence, creating an internal contradiction. The definition of promotional materials, specifically the development and approval process, is contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Use of Trademarks. Janssen and its Affiliates shall retain all right, title and interest in and to its and their respective Trademarks. Company shall Promote pursuant to this Agreement only under the Product name and other Product Trademarks used by Janssen in the Territory. Janssen hereby grants to Company, during the Term, a non-exclusive, royalty free right to use such Product name and Product Trademarks, and Janssen corporate names and logos, solely to the extent they are included on the Promotional Materials and solely for the purpose of using the Promotional Materials to Promote in the Territory under this Agreement. Company shall not, without the express, prior written consent of Janssen, alter or modify in any manner any Product Trademark or any other Trademark of Janssen. Company agrees to comply with such Janssen standard guidelines regarding the use of the Product Trademarks and any other Trademarks of Janssen, and any amendments thereto, as Janssen provides to Company from time to time after the Effective Date (including the Janssen Brand Usage Guidelines).
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'Marketing Toolkits' as potentially distinct from 'Promotional Materials' introduces a contradiction with Section 10.1. Section 10.1 grants Company limited trademark usage rights 'solely to the extent they are included on the Promotional Materials'. If 'Marketing Toolkits' are not considered 'Promotional Materials' as defined and controlled by Janssen, Company could develop and use 'Marketing Toolkits' that include Janssen's trademarks without explicit approval, directly violating Section 10.1. The altered text therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction and introduces uncertainty and ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Service Fee. In partial consideration of Company's Promotion of the Product in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2019 and Calendar Year 2020, Janssen shall pay Company a service fee (the "Service Fee"), as follows: (a) with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2019, an amount equal to (i) [***] percent ([***]%) of that portion of Cumulative Net Sales that is greater than the Baseline for Calendar Year 2019, less (ii) the total Service Fees that have been invoiced by Company to Janssen for all preceding Calendar Quarters of Calendar Year 2019; and (b) with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2020, an amount equal to (i) [***] percent ([***]%) of that portion of Cumulative Net Sales that is greater than the Baseline for Calendar Year 2020, less (ii) the total Service Fees that have been invoiced by Company to Janssen for all preceding Calendar Quarters of Calendar Year 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Service Fee. In partial consideration of Company's Promotion of the Product in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2019 and Calendar Year 2020, Janssen shall pay Company a service fee, also reffered to as Sales Comissions (the "Service Fee"), as follows: (a) with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2019, an amount equal to (i) [***] percent ([***]%) of that portion of Cumulative Net Sales that is greater than the Baseline for Calendar Year 2019, less (ii) the total Service Fees that have been invoiced by Company to Janssen for all preceding Calendar Quarters of Calendar Year 2019; and (b) with respect to each Calendar Quarter during Calendar Year 2020, an amount equal to (i) [***] percent ([***]%) of that portion of Cumulative Net Sales that is greater than the Baseline for Calendar Year 2020, less (ii) the total Service Fees that have been invoiced by Company to Janssen for all preceding Calendar Quarters of Calendar Year 2020.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Sales Commissions' as interchangeable with 'Service Fee' introduces a contradiction that impacts milestone payments. 'Service Fee' is calculated based on Cumulative Net Sales exceeding the Baseline. If 'Sales Commissions' are interpreted as something distinct and dependent on individual sales performance (not exceeding a Baseline), then the criteria for milestone achievements become unclear. This could mean company sales representative would push individual sales even over the baseline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Milestones. 5.3.1 In partial consideration of Company's Promotion of the Product in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Janssen shall pay Company milestone payments in accordance with this Section 5.3. Janssen shall notify Company in the applicable Fee Notice the first time the Cumulative Net Sales in the applicable Calendar Year exceed the amounts set forth in the following table (each, a "Milestone Event"). Janssen shall pay to Company the applicable milestone payments set forth in the table below (each, a "Milestone Payment") within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of an invoice from Company with respect to achievement of each Milestone Event. Each Milestone Payment shall be non-refundable and non-creditable. Milestone Event Milestone Payment Upon the first occasion that Cumulative Net Sales in Calendar Year 2019 exceed US$[***] US$[***] Upon the first occasion that Cumulative Net Sales in Calendar Year 2019 exceed US$[***] US$[***] Upon the first occasion that Cumulative Net Sales in Calendar Year 2019 exceed US$[***] US$[***] Upon the first occasion that Cumulative Net Sales in Calendar Year 2020 exceed US$[***] US$[***]
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Sales Commissions' as interchangeable with 'Service Fee'. The contradicted text specifies milestone payments based on Cumulative Net Sales exceeding certain thresholds. If 'Sales Commissions' are now also a consideration, and those commissions are not directly tied to the same Cumulative Net Sales thresholds, but rather to individual sales efforts potentially *over* the baseline as described in the explanation, it creates a contradiction. This is because the original milestone payments were clearly based on *overall* sales performance, while the introduction of commissions muddies the water, making it unclear whether achieving the milestone thresholds also satisfies the commission-related expectations. This directly impacts the criteria for achieving milestones, creating a strong contradiction within the document as the Milestone Event in 5.3 refers to Cumulative Net Sales and is contradicted by potentially including individual sales over the baseline when commissions are involved.
